compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
One plan defect currently mints up to N blocking findings via multi-criterion co-citation (ticket a879: two nonexistent symbols x five criteria = 10 blocking findings per round for 3 consecutive rounds; ticket c9d8 round 1: one underspecified escape-hatch surfaced as 4 blocking findings). Agents face an inflated block wall for a single fix-unit, and per-criterion block tallies are distorted for future calibration. Dedup must GROUP, never drop: the gate's strength is unchanged. NOTE: the existing `norm_id` in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py is criterion-SCOPED (its hash bakes in the sorted criteria list), so norm_id equality CANNOT group across criteria - a criteria-free key is required.

## What
- Add a `fix_unit_key(finding)` helper in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py, next to the norm_id machinery, with two NEW inline sub-helpers defined here (neither exists today): `_norm_location(f)` = the same significant-token normalization norm_id applies to text, applied to `f.get("location", "") or ""` (lowercase, `re.findall` token split, the existing stop-token filtering, sorted de-duplicated join) - token-set rather than exact-string because independent finder criteria format the same location differently (path:line vs prose section names); and `_norm_claim(f)` = norm_id's existing token-set normalization of the finding text, EXCLUDING the criteria list from the hash basis (norm_id bakes sorted criteria into its hash; that exclusion is the whole point). `fix_unit_key(f) = sha256(_norm_location(f) + "|" + _norm_claim(f))`. Same reword tolerance as norm_id, criteria-free.
- After verdict production and before the sidecar emit (the seam in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py where the rising/completion floors already run), group blocking findings sharing a fix_unit_key WITHOUT moving any finding out of verdict['blocking'] (build_payload at sidecar.py:421-427 concatenates the existing buckets; removing findings would silently drop them from the sidecar). Grouping only STAMPS fields: every grouped finding gains `group_id` (= the fix_unit_key) and `is_primary` (True on exactly one finding per group - highest priority, with deterministic tiebreak: missing priority sorts as 0.0; ties broken by alphabetically-first sorted criteria entry, then lowest finding id). The primary also gains `group_criteria` (the union of the group's criteria lists). All N findings keep decision=block - the gate's semantics, the verdict shape consumers see, and the sidecar's lossless capture are unchanged apart from the additive fields.
- Sidecar persistence: `build_payload`'s `_slim()` field-selection currently omits unknown fields - explicitly add `group_id`, `is_primary`, and `group_criteria` to the `_slim()` dict in sidecar.py so the stamps survive into the persisted payload (additive optional fields on the current plan-review sidecar schema).
- CLI: the renderer in src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py prints only primaries (suppressing findings with is_primary=False), each with a "(+N co-criteria: E4, G1G2, ...)" suffix where N = the group's folded-member count. Library/MCP consumers see the full stamped blocking list (no suppression outside the CLI renderer).
- Keep the change out of src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py: this is a pipeline-layer grouping, not a decision change.

## Scope
IN: fix_unit_key helper, grouping of BLOCKING findings only, group_id persistence through _slim()/build_payload, CLI rendering, tests. OUT: advisory grouping (the advisory cap already bounds volume), cross-round dedup (that is the rising floor's job), any threshold/posture change, any change to norm_id itself.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A synthetic verdict with 5 blocking findings citing the same location and same claim text across 5 different criteria: after grouping, verdict['blocking'] still contains all 5 findings, exactly 1 has is_primary=True, all share one group_id, and the primary's group_criteria is the 5-criteria union; the verdict remains BLOCK. Oracle: new unit test; `python -m pytest tests/unit -k "fix_unit or group" -x` green.
- [ ] Findings at different locations or with different claims are NOT grouped, and two findings with identical text but different criteria ARE grouped (the criteria-free key property). Oracle: unit tests over fix_unit_key asserting both directions.
- [ ] The CLI output for a grouped primary finding includes the suffix "(+N co-criteria: <union>)" where N is the number of folded findings. Oracle: unit test over the renderer in src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py; `python -m pytest tests/unit -k "group" -x` green.
- [ ] The emitted sidecar retains all 5 findings with decision=block, each carrying the same group_id and exactly one carrying is_primary=True. Oracle: unit test reads the build_payload output and asserts those three properties.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all pass.
compiled_state.title: Cross-criterion fix-unit grouping for blocking findings (one defect, one block group)
